scenarios.title: Utility + Memory Bootstrap
scenarios.goal: Demonstrate deterministic utility tool usage for calculations, doc visibility, and persistent memory.
scenarios.notes: Use this as the first scenario in stakeholder demos to prove control and reliability before RAG-heavy tasks.

turns.prompt: List the indexed documents and group them into contracts, security/compliance, runbooks, and API references.
Assume annual platform spend is 2,760,000 USD. Calculate 17.5% risk reserve and monthly reserve. Show both results.
Remember these values exactly: target_jurisdiction=England and Wales; risk_reserve_monthly_usd=40250; executive_demo_owner=Platform Reliability.
What are all saved memory keys, and what value is stored for target_jurisdiction?

scenarios.title: RAG Resolution and Search Strategy Switching
scenarios.goal: Show title resolution, scoped search, cross-document retrieval, and strategy shifts for non-trivial prompts.
scenarios.notes: Prompts intentionally use partial file names and synonym drift to force resolution + mixed retrieval behavior.

turns.prompt: Using the release notes and integrations docs, identify what changed around tool-calling reliability and why those changes matter to production operations. Cite precisely.
Search across all KB documents for policy language that describes retention boundaries and deletion windows, then reconcile any wording differences into one policy summary with citations.
I remember a document about integrations and tool schemas, but not the exact file name. Resolve it, then explain when keyword search is preferable to vector search in this codebase context.

scenarios.title: Clause Navigation and Precise Extraction
scenarios.goal: Demonstrate structure discovery followed by exact clause extraction across a long legal-style document.
scenarios.notes: Designed to force structure-aware retrieval instead of broad semantic search.

turns.prompt: From 06_master_services_agreement_v1.md, extract and summarize Clause 6, Clause 9, and Clause 14 with exact obligations and penalties. Include citations for each clause.
From that same agreement, pull Clause 11.2, Clause 11.3, and Clause 12.1 and explain how they interact from an operational risk perspective.

scenarios.title: Requirements Traceability Review
scenarios.goal: Exercise requirement extraction and filtered evidence retrieval for compliance traceability.
scenarios.notes: Prompts require identification of SHALL/MUST/REQ statements and filtered constraint mapping.

turns.prompt: In 09_ai_ops_control_standard.md, extract all requirements related to model release gates, approval workflow, and rollback obligations. Return them as REQ-mapped bullets with citations.
Now extract requirements from 11_vendor_security_schedule.md that apply to encryption, key management, and incident notification timing. Highlight any explicit deadlines.

scenarios.title: Structural Diff Across Contract Versions
scenarios.goal: Show document-structure diff and identify newly introduced, removed, or renumbered obligations.
scenarios.notes: This scenario is best for showing how the agent handles large legal documents and version drift.

turns.prompt: Run a structural diff between 06_master_services_agreement_v1.md and 07_master_services_agreement_v2.md. Identify clauses only in v2, clauses removed from v1, and clauses that were split or merged.
Based on the structural diff, produce a change log for legal review with three categories: scope expansion, risk tightening, and operational burden increase.

scenarios.title: Clause-by-Clause Conflict Review
scenarios.goal: Demonstrate side-by-side clause comparison and contradiction analysis with intermediate reasoning state.
scenarios.notes: Built to encourage multiple compare calls and scratchpad accumulation before final synthesis.

turns.prompt: Compare Clause 5, Clause 8, Clause 11, and Clause 15 between 06_master_services_agreement_v1.md and 07_master_services_agreement_v2.md. For each clause, state whether v2 is stricter, looser, or materially different.
Now focus only on contradictions between security obligations in the same two documents and produce a remediation negotiation checklist with citations.

scenarios.title: Parallel Multi-Document Risk Board
scenarios.goal: Trigger supervisor fan-out/fan-in via parallel_rag and synthesize a unified cross-document risk board.
scenarios.notes: Use this to highlight enterprise-scale orchestration rather than single-document Q&A.

turns.prompt: Build a unified risk board by comparing obligations and failure-handling guidance across 08_data_processing_addendum_global.md, 09_ai_ops_control_standard.md, and 10_incident_communications_playbook.md. Return top 10 risks with severity, owner, and evidence citations.
For the same three documents, produce a 30-60-90 day remediation roadmap and justify each milestone with citations.

scenarios.title: Executive Due Diligence Grand Finale
scenarios.goal: Show end-to-end utility + RAG + memory orchestration in one executive briefing flow.
scenarios.notes: Recommended final demo for manager walkthrough: deterministic setup, evidence-heavy analysis, and concise recommendation.

turns.prompt: Remember this executive context: board_priority=regulatory resilience, budget_ceiling_usd=1900000, launch_window=Q3 2026.

Using 06_master_services_agreement_v2.md, 08_data_processing_addendum_global.md, and 11_vendor_security_schedule.md, produce an executive due-diligence brief: top obligations, top breach exposure vectors, and top negotiation asks with citations.
Now calculate a proposed reserve equal to 12.5% of budget_ceiling_usd and map that reserve to the top three mitigation workstreams you just identified.
Finish with a board-ready recommendation in three sections: proceed conditions, blockers, and immediate next actions. Use citations and reference the remembered launch_window.
